documentation.content:
Post ingestion is degraded or dead.

Conditions (any):
- indexed rate < 5 posts/s for 30 min (healthy floor is ~15-25/s)
- posts-indexed metric absent for 20 min (worker down or all flushes wedged)
- post consumer cursor lag > 30 min for 30 min (replay livelock: flushing but stuck in the past)

Playbook:
1. `gcloud logging read 'resource.labels.service_name="jetstream-indexer"' --project=timelines-492720 --freshness=1h` — look for flush_failed / queue_drop / embed_backoff / ws_error storms on the post consumer.
2. Check bsky-db for a stuck autovacuum or IO-bound inserts: pg_stat_activity wait_event DataFileRead on INSERT INTO bsky.posts; pg_stat_progress_vacuum. A vacuum stuck >12h in 'vacuuming indexes' on the HNSW index starves inserts - cancel it (pg_cancel_backend) and consider scripts/reclaim-posts-bloat.ts (REINDEX).
3. Restart the service if wedged: `gcloud run services update jetstream-indexer --region=us-central1 --project=timelines-492720 --update-env-vars=RESTARTED_AT=<now>`.

See the 2026-07-07 outage postmortem in the PR that added this alert.
